findings.line: Jaina rode through ruts pressed deep into the old road, her Thalassian mare sweating in the cold while orchard walls and trimmed ditches insisted that someone still paid to keep this land neat.
findings.reason: Opening section repeats medium-length subject-verb setup followed by clipped interpretive fragments across consecutive paragraphs.

findings.line: Caer Darrow rose out of the mist by pieces. First the seawall. Then towers. Then the keep itself.
findings.reason: Arrival sequence keeps using declarative sentence plus stacked fragments in the same rhythm.

findings.line: The room went back to itself. Dark lake. Warm walls. Hidden wards in the plaster.
findings.reason: Late chapter again falls into sentence-then-fragment cadence; the pattern repeats instead of varying.

findings.line: Jandice glanced at her father, then back.
findings.reason: Glanced-then-back is interchangeable stage business; it does not reveal anything specific about Jandice beyond basic turn-taking.

findings.line: Jandice's eyes widened a fraction at the lack of hesitation. Then she smiled again.
findings.reason: Widened and smiled again are stock reaction beats that could belong to almost anyone in the scene.

findings.line: Jandice glanced back at it with proprietary fondness.
findings.reason: The beat is tied to the glowing fungi and to Jandice's possessive attachment to the place; it is not a generic emotional label.

findings.line: She could almost hear Sylvanas asking how long everything would take.
findings.reason: Deletion tightens the thought to direct recollection; the hedge is padding.

findings.line: The oars entered the water with almost no sound.
findings.reason: The hedge weakens a simple sensory image that becomes stronger without it.

findings.line: It might blur the edges enough to cost an eavesdropper certainty.
findings.reason: The uncertainty is the practical point of the action; Jaina is estimating ward efficacy before taking a risk.

findings.line: That made it worse.
findings.reason: The previous lines already show why actual workers inside the walls are morally worse than a crude prison-theater image.

findings.line: Routine use. It would have been easier if the place had wanted to leer.
findings.reason: The second sentence translates the unsettling image instead of trusting the tidy workrooms and copied notes to carry it.

findings.line: The next rooms sharpened the method instead of broadening the horror.
findings.reason: Paragraph opens with a thesis, supplies examples, then lands on a summarizing gloss.

findings.line: The same answer kept returning in different language.
findings.reason: Paragraph begins with a claim, inventories evidence, and interprets the result instead of staying in the live act of reading.

findings.line: Recognition hunger. Not approval. Not charm.
findings.reason: Paragraph-final gloss names the emotional meaning instead of ending on Jandice brightening.

findings.line: No chaos. No theatrical depravity. Only intellect with all the softness planed out of it.
findings.reason: Scene refuses to stop at the concrete rooms and instead supplies a final interpretive slogan.

findings.line: Open route, Jaina thought. Trap all the same.
findings.reason: Scene-ending summary closes the emotional logic instead of leaving the descent on image or action.

audits.dominantPattern: Pattern 5/6 — buildings and institutions given intention

findings.line: orchard walls and trimmed ditches insisted that someone still paid to keep this land neat.
findings.subject: orchard walls and trimmed ditches
findings.reason: Pattern 1/6. Infrastructure carries a human judgment the visible labor should carry.

findings.line: House Barov fed half a province and meant to keep doing it.
findings.subject: House Barov
findings.reason: Pattern 5. Institution gets desire and intention instead of naming the people maintaining output.

findings.line: How long back out if the house smiled and closed behind her.
findings.subject: house
findings.realAgent: guards, servants, hospitality ritual, physical gate control
findings.reason: Pattern 5. The estate is given social intent and trap awareness.

findings.line: enough Kirin Tor authority to make a noble house hesitate before it lied.
findings.subject: house
findings.realAgent: Barov staff and family deciding what to say
findings.reason: Pattern 4/5. An institution is made into the liar instead of the nobles inside it.

findings.line: Good. Let the house react first.
findings.subject: house
findings.reason: Pattern 5. The sentence launders the response onto the estate instead of the people at the gate.

findings.line: The house had been waiting for this knock, then.
findings.subject: house
findings.realAgent: Alexei, Jandice, and the staff prepared for scrutiny
findings.reason: Pattern 5. Building-level anticipation replaces concrete human preparation.

findings.line: The building admitted it by weight even while the tour denied it by direction.
findings.subject: building / tour
findings.realAgent: ward pressure, floor plan, and Jandice's guided route choices
findings.reason: Pattern 6/7. Architecture and route are doing argumentative work that belongs to mechanics and human curation.

findings.line: Which meant the room was almost certainly listening.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: embedded wards and whoever monitors them
findings.reason: Pattern 7. The pressure source is surveillance magic, not the room itself.

findings.line: Jaina stood still for a count of ten and let the chamber declare itself.
findings.subject: chamber
findings.realAgent: ward lines, vibration threads, visible fixtures
findings.reason: Pattern 6. The room is given self-description instead of the prose naming the evidence Jaina reads.

findings.line: The building disliked being spoken through.
findings.subject: building
findings.reason: Pattern 5. Inanimate architecture is assigned preference and irritation.

findings.line: I assumed that when the lake announced me.
findings.subject: lake
findings.realAgent: outer ward line and whatever signal it sent inward
findings.reason: Pattern 2/3. Geography is made to send a message the wards actually sent.

findings.line: Dalaran saw the gate and declared the field forbidden so it would never have to learn what lay beyond it.
findings.subject: Dalaran
findings.realAgent: Council members and Kirin Tor authorities
findings.reason: Pattern 4. Institutional self-deception is assigned to the city-name instead of specific people.

findings.line: The lake carried a chemical cold.
findings.subject: lake
findings.reason: Literal non-human action. The sentence describes physical conditions, not intention.

audits.registerSummary: Jaina thinks like a young battle-ready scholar: she notices wards, logistics, tailoring, reagent marks, routes, and operational risk. Her interiority is sharp and tactical, but usually concrete. She can be dry and incisive, but she is not naturally a slogan machine or an omniscient caption writer while moving through a live threat space.
audits.structuralHabit: yes — caption fragments recur at scene pivots and paragraph endings

findings.line: A good horse, road-bred and steady around ward hum.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence reads like an authored caption pinned over the mare rather than Jaina's live tactical thought.

findings.line: A man who signed things and let the world obey.
findings.povMismatch: This is a polished conceptual compression of class power, not the concrete social read Jaina would likely think in that instant.

findings.line: Scholar's hands.
findings.povMismatch: Verdict-tag fragment labels the previous physical evidence instead of staying inside the act of noticing it.

findings.line: Curated, then.
findings.povMismatch: Compressed judgment fragment lands like a caption over the porter reveal.

findings.line: Real enough to trust. Dangerous enough to follow.
findings.povMismatch: Paired slogan fragments package Jandice into a thesis statement rather than a thought in motion.

findings.line: Recognition. She wanted it badly enough for the need to show.
findings.povMismatch: The abstract noun plus polished motive summary sound written about Jandice rather than thought by Jaina inside the exchange.

findings.line: The room was comfortable to the point of insult.
findings.povMismatch: Possible in-character attitude, but the phrasing reads like a crafted line rather than the immediate sensory scan that follows.

findings.line: There should have been triumph in that. Jaina got none from it. Only the clean hard drop of initiative leaving her hands.
findings.povMismatch: The conceptual phrasing of initiative leaving her hands is too polished and detached for a live response beat.

findings.line: No chaos. No theatrical depravity. Only intellect with all the softness planed out of it.
findings.povMismatch: This is a thesis-fragment stack that sounds like a chapter epigraph, not Jaina's embodied perception.

findings.line: Work.
findings.povMismatch: Single-word verdict fragment captions the corridor instead of letting the clang and silence speak.

findings.line: Open route, Jaina thought. Trap all the same.
findings.povMismatch: The closing fragment reads like a neat authorial tag placed on the end of the scene.

findings.line: Window. Desk. Ward-thread in the lintel. Another in the lamp base.
findings.povMismatch: None; this is a concrete tactical inventory in her natural register.

clusterWarnings: Pattern 2 fragment pivots recur across narration and dialogue
Negated fragment rhythm is concentrated in Jaina/Jandice analytic exchanges

findings.line: Not identical handwriting. Kin handwriting. Lineage handwriting.
findings.reason: Pattern 2. The negative half is dead-weight setup for the real claim.

findings.line: Maybe not for her specifically. For a version of her.
findings.reason: Pattern 3. The sentence refuses the real target before naming it.

findings.line: The strongest signature still did not come from the visible keep. It came from under it.
findings.reason: Pattern 5. The negated preamble delays the real location.

findings.line: Not the layered hum of Dalaran's vaults. Pressure with teeth in it.
findings.reason: Pattern 2. The fragment pivot exists only to stage the replacement image.

findings.line: Not the whole truth.
findings.reason: Pattern 2. The fragment is decorative withholding; the surrounding speech survives without it.

findings.line: Not imitation. Continuation.
findings.reason: Pattern 2. The negative first beat only dramatizes the direct claim.

findings.line: The body was not passive matter waiting for command from outside. The body learned.
findings.reason: Pattern 5. The negated setup is scaffolding for the real statement.

findings.line: Recognition hunger. Not approval. Not charm.
findings.reason: Pattern 7. Chained negation builds a hallway to the already-named meaning.

findings.line: No chaos. No theatrical depravity. Only intellect with all the softness planed out of it.
findings.deletionTest: Only intellect with all the softness planed out of it.
findings.reason: Pattern 8. Mundane reveal chain uses two rejected nouns to stage the final label.

findings.line: No screams. No chanting. Work.
findings.reason: Pattern 8. Two negated beats theatrically clear the runway for the real noun.

findings.line: Not entirely.
findings.reason: Pattern 2. The fragment withholds the real state only to make the follow-up land harder.

findings.line: Do not trust memory to be kinder tomorrow than it is tonight.
findings.reason: Procedural imperative. It is an instruction, not a contrast construction.

findings.line: permission is not the same as protection.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic in active decision-making. The distinction changes how Jaina should read the descent.

summary.verdictReason: 47 KILL verdicts across 4 guides: GPT-5 prose issues 14, intent smear 12, narrator seep 11, negation addiction 10.
